In Parliament

Andrew’s speeches, debate interventions and questions to Ministers.

In Parliament
Image
Sir Andrew Mitchell pushes for a clear national accident prevention strategy
28 April 2026

Sir Andrew Mitchell leads a Westminster Hall debate on the importance of developing a clear and comprehensive national accident prevention strategy.
Image
Sir Andrew Mitchell presses Government to get tough on overseas territories over beneficial ownership registers
21 April 2026

Sir Andrew Mitchell calls on the Government to end the delay and take a tougher stance against those overseas territories that still have not established publicly accessible registers of beneficial ownership as required under the Sanctions and Anti-Money Laundering Act 2018.
Image
Sir Andrew Mitchell calls out PM for shifting blame onto Sir Olly Robbins in Mandelson vetting scandal
20 April 2026

Former Deputy Foreign Secretary Sir Andrew Mitchell challenges the Prime Minister for attempting to shift responsibility onto senior civil servant Sir Olly Robbins for the vetting controversy surrounding Peter Mandelson’s appointment as UK Ambassador to the United States.
Image
Sir Andrew Mitchell calls for immediate uplift in defence spending
13 April 2026

Former Deputy Foreign Secretary Sir Andrew Mitchell urges the PM to commit to an immediate uplift in defence spending towards a 5% increase so that the UK can play a full part in European defence.
Image
Sir Andrew Mitchell criticises PM’s “anaemic” response to US strikes as out of step with Five Eyes
2 March 2026

Former Deputy Foreign Secretary Sir Andrew Mitchell criticises the Prime Minister's response last week as weak and disappointing, and out of step with other Five Eyes nations. He urges review of shadow Attorney General Lord Wolfson KC's analysis, which argues British active support for allied strikes is fully consistent with international law.
Image
Written Parliamentary Questions – February 2026
28 February 2026

Written questions allow MPs to ask for information on the work, policy and activities of Government departments, related bodies, and the administration of Parliament.
Image
Sir Andrew Mitchell urges Government to focus on clemency for Jimmy Lai
9 February 2026

Former Deputy Foreign Secretary Sir Andrew Mitchell calls on the Government to focus 100% on securing clemency for Jimmy Lai and a one-way ticket back to the UK and to his family.
Image
Sir Andrew Mitchell seeks PM’s assurance that release of Jimmy Lai was raised on China visit
2 February 2026

Former Deputy Foreign Secretary Sir Andrew Mitchell welcomes the Prime Minister’s visit to China and seeks assurances from the PM that he personally raised the case for clemency for 78-year-old British citizen Jimmy Lai who is being held in solitary confinement in Hong Kong.
Image
Written Parliamentary Questions – January 2026
31 January 2026

Written questions allow MPs to ask for information on the work, policy and activities of Government departments, related bodies, and the administration of Parliament.

Rt Hon Sir Andrew Mitchell MP
House of Commons
London SW1A 0AA

Westminster Office: 020 7219 8516

Constituency Office: 0121 354 2229

Email: andrew.mitchell.mp@parliament.uk

 

Sutton Coldfield Constituency: Sutton Four Oaks, Sutton Mere Green, Sutton Reddicap, Sutton Roughley, Sutton Trinity, Sutton Vesey, Sutton Walmley & Minworth, Sutton Wylde Green.
